title: Mesa Redonda 1 — 'Tesoros Naturales' (Spanish 202 prep, Colombia birds)
html:
TL;DR

  • A second Emily schoolwork-help artifact within 48 hours of the deforestation essay — this one is Mesa Redonda 1: "Tesoros Naturales" for ESPAÑOL 202, a roundtable presentation prompt covering Colombia, Ecuador, or Venezuela.
  • Country pick: Colombia — 52 million population, more bird species than any other country, many endemic, valued by pueblos originarios for cultural/symbolic meaning.
  • Yahir is drafting talking-points (5–9 vocabulary words, no full sentences allowed per the rubric) — bullet form, presentation-ready: Aves viven en muchas regiones de colombia / Como la selva, montañas y costas / Las aves son importantes para el ecosistema.
  • Pattern continuation of the Emily arc: Yahir as quiet co-author on her university coursework — same posture as the Billy vocab list and the FHS issue brief.
  • The rubric itself is preserved in the doc (4 image slides on ubicación, descripción, importancia ecosistémica, valor simbólico-cultural for pueblos originarios) — useful template if Emily gets another mesa redonda.

The content

Bullet-grade Spanish:

  • Pobalacion de columbia es aproximadamente cincuenta y dos milliones
  • Colombia tiene mas especies de aves que cualquier otro país
  • Muchas aves son endémicas
  • Viven en selvas, montañas y costas
  • Son famosas por sus colores y cantos
  • Para muchos pueblos originarios, las aves tienen valor cultural y simbólico

Two oceans noted at the top — Colombia is one of the few South American countries with both Pacific and Caribbean coastline (a non-trivial trivia hook for a 5-minute presentation).

Why this matters to the graph

The Emily arc has an unmistakable "Yahir helps Emily with school" sub-pattern. By Feb 5, three different course-help artifacts exist within the vault: vocab list (Jan 29), deforestation essay (Feb 3), mesa redonda prep (Feb 5). The cadence is roughly one piece of help per week through early Feb 2026. This is a love-language inventory worth surfacing if/when the arc page gets densified into a synthesis.
